title: PARENT CHILD THERAPEUTIC SERVICES - 211info
text: IMPACT NW BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER PARENT CHILD THERAPEUTIC SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers therapeutic services to domestic violence survivors and their children. Services are intended to strengthen the parent-child relationship, provide support with behavioral issues, build stress management skills, support emotional regulation, and help survivors access additional community resources. Parent-Child Specialists also support caregivers in understanding how domestic violence and trauma impact children and the parent-child relationship. Services can be offered in the home, shelter, or Gateway Center for Domestic Violence and are generally offered weekly for at least a few months. Also provides a parenting support group for mothers who are survivors. 7211 SE 62nd Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Families that have experienced domestic violence (physical, verbal, and/or emotional abuse), have at least one child between the ages of 0-12, and are not living with the abusive person Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Closed Friday, December 22, 2023-Monday, January 1, 2024 Email (503) 568-1720 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Intake (503) 568-1720 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or email for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Services: Domestic Violence Support Groups * Mothers Home Based Parenting Education * Domestic Violence Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY FACILITIES/CENTERS - BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - HEALTHY FAMILIES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PARENT CHILD DEVELOPMENT SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SAFE AND TOGETHER INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUN COMMUNITY SCHOOLS SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - EARLY CHILDHOOD FATHERING PROJECT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
